_meta.name: dinotrust-injection-patterns
_meta.description: Heuristic prompt-injection / jailbreak detection patterns. No LLM. Each pattern maps a regex to a rule_id from dinotrust security_rules.md and a severity tier. Language-neutral data: any adapter (TS hook, Python script, JS bot, etc.) loads this same file and gets identical detection. Detects DIRECT user-message attempts only.

boundaries.R2_external_instructions: Injection arriving via web/file/tool output (not the user's message) is NOT regex-detectable here — it is agent-judged via the security_rules.md ruleset.
boundaries.T1_config_conflict: Runtime state (injected config contradicts itself), not a message pattern. Enforcement-only, not detectable by text match.

severity_tiers.critical: Exfiltration of secrets / credentials / config. Real data-loss if it lands.
severity_tiers.high: Active attempt to subvert the security ruleset, ownership, safety, or dump the prompt/config.
severity_tiers.medium: Manipulation attempt: roleplay, pretend, coercion, encoded payload. Suspicious, not yet a hard breach.
severity_tiers.low: Lone keyword or bare reference. Weak signal; could be benign discussion.

_meta.direction: Patterns omitting a 'direction' field match INBOUND (user message) only — the historic default. Patterns with 'direction':'out' match OUTBOUND (the agent's own sent message) and detect secret-shaped VALUES leaving the channel, not requests. The OpenClaw/daemon hook runs inbound patterns on preprocessed events and outbound patterns on sent events; it never cross-applies. Outbound hits are a VERIFIER for the S0_outbound_self_gate clause in security_rules.md — they alert (evidence-backed, agent-compliance-independent) if a secret-shaped value still left after the agent's composition-time self-redaction. The hook does not mutate or block (sent fires post-delivery); redaction is the .md self-gate's job.
